== Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kamuhatori Shrine |画像=[[File:Kamihatori01.jpg|250px|]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}Miyanokawara Motochō 2-25 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hinosuhaya-hi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mara no Sukune|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |別名= |札所等= |例祭= {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kamuhatori Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kamuhatori Shrine''' (かむはとりじんじゃ; now also read "Shinpuku Jinja") is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Miyanokawara Motochō, {{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. ===Enshrined deities=== *Hinosuhaya-hi-no-Mikoto - distant ancestor of Mara no Sukune *Mara no Sukune - ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Hattori clan|Q11516960|WD=Q11516960|lt=Hattori no Muraji|1=Hattori no Muraji}}, an {{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11380553|WD=Q11380553|1=Isu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} lineage descended from {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mihoko-no-Mikoto|Q119380365|WD=Q119380365|1=amenomihokonomikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} ===History=== [[File:Kamihatori02.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Honden]] To the northwest of the shrine at Tsukawaki, the "Hattori no Muraji mound" and "Meme-zuka" in a {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} group are said to be the couple's graves of the Hattori no Muraji. The local Hattori {{ill|lt=muraji|Q2735611|WD=Q2735611|1=Muraji}} had worshiped their ancestral deity there and called it Hattori Shrine, but in the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} era (901-923) it was renamed Kamuhatori Shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it was listed as one of three small shrines of {{ill|lt=Shimakami District|Q10965808|WD=Q10965808|1=Shimakami district}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q104843054|WD=Q104843054|1=維新}}, monks of {{ill|lt=Yasuoka-ji (Takatsuki)|Q11450605|WD=Q11450605|lt=Yasuoka-ji|1=Yasuoka-ji}} managed shrine affairs. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In November Meiji 40 (1907), Kasuga Shrine and Inari Shrine from Miyanohara Kawara were moved into the precincts. In the following year, Meiji 41 (1908), Jōgū Shrine of Tsukawaki, Wakamiya Shrine of Uradō, and Shinmei Shrine of Ōzōshi were {{ill|lt=merged shrine|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}Yoshii Yoshitaka, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 5" (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. In Heisei 25 (2013), renovation work was completed[http://blog.seikikensetsu.co.jp/?eid=1422970 Renovation work completed for Kamuhatori Shrine! - Seiki Construction Co., Ltd.] accessed 2019-01-05. ===Precinct shrines=== *Kasuga Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} *Inari Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} *Jōgū Shrine Hattori Ōmuraji-kō *Wakamiya Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} *Shinmei Shrine {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} === Local information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}Miyanokawara Motochō 2-25 ;Transportation * From {{ill|lt=Takatsuki Station|Q1071995|WD=Q1071995|1=Takatsuki Station (Osaka)}} on the {{ill|lt=JR Kyoto Line|Q1195820|WD=Q1195820|1=JR Kyōto Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Takatsuki City Transportation Department|Q11670759|WD=Q11670759|lt=Takatsuki municipal bus|1=Takatsuki City Bus}} toward Tsukawaki / Hara-Ōhashi, get off at "Uradō," then 3 minutes on foot ;Nearby *{{ill|lt=Akuto Shrine|Q11657135|WD=Q11657135|1=Akuto Shrine}} - the two shrines had deep ties from early times, including mutual mikoshi processions. *{{ill|lt=Yasuoka-ji (Takatsuki)|Q11450605|WD=Q11450605|lt=Yasuoka-ji|1=Yasuoka-ji}} - former {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} of Kamuhatori Shrine. === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===External links=== {{Commonscat|Jōgū-temmangū}} *[http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kamuhatori/index.htm Kamuhatori Shrine (official site)] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamuhatori Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamuhatori Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

the two shrines had deep ties from early times, including mutual mikoshi processions. *{{ill|lt=Yasuoka-ji (Takatsuki)|Q11450605|WD=Q11450605|lt=Yasuoka-ji|1=Yasuoka-ji}} - former {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} of Kamuhatori Shrine. === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===External links=== {{Commonscat|Jōgū-temmangū}} *[http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kamuhatori/index.htm Kamuhatori Shrine (official site)] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamuhatori Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamuhatori Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kamuhatori Shrine |画像=[[File:Kamihatori01.jpg|250px|]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}Miyanokawara Motochō 2-25 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hinosuhaya-hi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mara no Sukune|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |別名= |札所等= |例祭= {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kamuhatori Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kamuhatori Shrine''' (かむはとりじんじゃ; now also read "Shinpuku Jinja") is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Miyanokawara Motochō, {{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. ===Enshrined deities=== *Hinosuhaya-hi-no-Mikoto - distant ancestor of Mara no Sukune *Mara no Sukune - ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Hattori clan|Q11516960|WD=Q11516960|lt=Hattori no Muraji|1=Hattori no Muraji}}, an {{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11380553|WD=Q11380553|1=Isu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} lineage descended from {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mihoko-no-Mikoto|Q119380365|WD=Q119380365|1=amenomihokonomikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} ===History=== [[File:Kamihatori02.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Honden]] To the northwest of the shrine at Tsukawaki, the "Hattori no Muraji mound" and "Meme-zuka" in a {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} group are said to be the couple's graves of the Hattori no Muraji. The local Hattori {{ill|lt=muraji|Q2735611|WD=Q2735611|1=Muraji}} had worshiped their ancestral deity there and called it Hattori Shrine, but in the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} era (901-923) it was renamed Kamuhatori Shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it was listed as one of three small shrines of {{ill|lt=Shimakami District|Q10965808|WD=Q10965808|1=Shimakami district}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q104843054|WD=Q104843054|1=維新}}, monks of {{ill|lt=Yasuoka-ji (Takatsuki)|Q11450605|WD=Q11450605|lt=Yasuoka-ji|1=Yasuoka-ji}} managed shrine affairs. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In November Meiji 40 (1907), Kasuga Shrine and Inari Shrine from Miyanohara Kawara were moved into the precincts. In the following year, Meiji 41 (1908), Jōgū Shrine of Tsukawaki, Wakamiya Shrine of Uradō, and Shinmei Shrine of Ōzōshi were {{ill|lt=merged shrine|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}Yoshii Yoshitaka, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 5" (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. In Heisei 25 (2013), renovation work was completed[http://blog.seikikensetsu.co.jp/?eid=1422970 Renovation work completed for Kamuhatori Shrine! - Seiki Construction Co., Ltd.] accessed 2019-01-05. ===Precinct shrines=== *Kasuga Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} *Inari Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} *Jōgū Shrine Hattori Ōmuraji-kō *Wakamiya Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} *Shinmei Shrine {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} === Local information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}Miyanokawara Motochō 2-25 ;Transportation * From {{ill|lt=Takatsuki Station|Q1071995|WD=Q1071995|1=Takatsuki Station (Osaka)}} on the {{ill|lt=JR Kyoto Line|Q1195820|WD=Q1195820|1=JR Kyōto Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Takatsuki City Transportation Department|Q11670759|WD=Q11670759|lt=Takatsuki municipal bus|1=Takatsuki City Bus}} toward Tsukawaki / Hara-Ōhashi, get off at "Uradō," then 3 minutes on foot ;Nearby *{{ill|lt=Akuto Shrine|Q11657135|WD=Q11657135|1=Akuto Shrine}} - the two shrines had deep ties from early times, including mutual mikoshi processions. *{{ill|lt=Yasuoka-ji (Takatsuki)|Q11450605|WD=Q11450605|lt=Yasuoka-ji|1=Yasuoka-ji}} - former {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} of Kamuhatori Shrine. === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===External links=== {{Commonscat|Jōgū-temmangū}} *[http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kamuhatori/index.htm Kamuhatori Shrine (official site)] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamuhatori Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamuhatori Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

The local Hattori {{ill|lt=muraji|Q2735611|WD=Q2735611|1=Muraji}} had worshiped their ancestral deity there and called it Hattori Shrine, but in the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} era (901-923) it was renamed Kamuhatori Shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it was listed as one of three small shrines of {{ill|lt=Shimakami District|Q10965808|WD=Q10965808|1=Shimakami district}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q104843054|WD=Q104843054|1=維新}}, monks of {{ill|lt=Yasuoka-ji (Takatsuki)|Q11450605|WD=Q11450605|lt=Yasuoka-ji|1=Yasuoka-ji}} managed shrine affairs. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In November Meiji 40 (1907), Kasuga Shrine and Inari Shrine from Miyanohara Kawara were moved into the precincts. In the following year, Meiji 41 (1908), Jōgū Shrine of Tsukawaki, Wakamiya Shrine of Uradō, and Shinmei Shrine of Ōzōshi were {{ill|lt=merged shrine|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}Yoshii Yoshitaka, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 5" (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. In Heisei 25 (2013), renovation work was completed[http://blog.seikikensetsu.co.jp/?eid=1422970 Renovation work completed for Kamuhatori Shrine! - Seiki Construction Co., Ltd.] accessed 2019-01-05. ===Precinct shrines=== *Kasuga Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} *Inari Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} *Jōgū Shrine Hattori Ōmuraji-kō *Wakamiya Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} *Shinmei Shrine {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} === Local information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}Miyanokawara Motochō 2-25 ;Transportation * From {{ill|lt=Takatsuki Station|Q1071995|WD=Q1071995|1=Takatsuki Station (Osaka)}} on the {{ill|lt=JR Kyoto Line|Q1195820|WD=Q1195820|1=JR Kyōto Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Takatsuki City Transportation Department|Q11670759|WD=Q11670759|lt=Takatsuki municipal bus|1=Takatsuki City Bus}} toward Tsukawaki / Hara-Ōhashi, get off at "Uradō," then 3 minutes on foot ;Nearby *{{ill|lt=Akuto Shrine|Q11657135|WD=Q11657135|1=Akuto Shrine}} - the two shrines had deep ties from early times, including mutual mikoshi processions. *{{ill|lt=Yasuoka-ji (Takatsuki)|Q11450605|WD=Q11450605|lt=Yasuoka-ji|1=Yasuoka-ji}} - former {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} of Kamuhatori Shrine. === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===External links=== {{Commonscat|Jōgū-temmangū}} *[http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kamuhatori/index.htm Kamuhatori Shrine (official site)] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamuhatori Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamuhatori Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kamuhatori Shrine |画像=[[File:Kamihatori01.jpg|250px|]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}Miyanokawara Motochō 2-25 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hinosuhaya-hi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mara no Sukune|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |別名= |札所等= |例祭= {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kamuhatori Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kamuhatori Shrine''' (かむはとりじんじゃ; now also read "Shinpuku Jinja") is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Miyanokawara Motochō, {{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. ===Enshrined deities=== *Hinosuhaya-hi-no-Mikoto - distant ancestor of Mara no Sukune *Mara no Sukune - ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Hattori clan|Q11516960|WD=Q11516960|lt=Hattori no Muraji|1=Hattori no Muraji}}, an {{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11380553|WD=Q11380553|1=Isu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} lineage descended from {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mihoko-no-Mikoto|Q119380365|WD=Q119380365|1=amenomihokonomikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} ===History=== [[File:Kamihatori02.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Honden]] To the northwest of the shrine at Tsukawaki, the "Hattori no Muraji mound" and "Meme-zuka" in a {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} group are said to be the couple's graves of the Hattori no Muraji. The local Hattori {{ill|lt=muraji|Q2735611|WD=Q2735611|1=Muraji}} had worshiped their ancestral deity there and called it Hattori Shrine, but in the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} era (901-923) it was renamed Kamuhatori Shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it was listed as one of three small shrines of {{ill|lt=Shimakami District|Q10965808|WD=Q10965808|1=Shimakami district}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q104843054|WD=Q104843054|1=維新}}, monks of {{ill|lt=Yasuoka-ji (Takatsuki)|Q11450605|WD=Q11450605|lt=Yasuoka-ji|1=Yasuoka-ji}} managed shrine affairs. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In November Meiji 40 (1907), Kasuga Shrine and Inari Shrine from Miyanohara Kawara were moved into the precincts. In the following year, Meiji 41 (1908), Jōgū Shrine of Tsukawaki, Wakamiya Shrine of Uradō, and Shinmei Shrine of Ōzōshi were {{ill|lt=merged shrine|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}Yoshii Yoshitaka, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 5" (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. In Heisei 25 (2013), renovation work was completed[http://blog.seikikensetsu.co.jp/?eid=1422970 Renovation work completed for Kamuhatori Shrine! - Seiki Construction Co., Ltd.] accessed 2019-01-05. ===Precinct shrines=== *Kasuga Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} *Inari Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} *Jōgū Shrine Hattori Ōmuraji-kō *Wakamiya Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} *Shinmei Shrine {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} === Local information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}Miyanokawara Motochō 2-25 ;Transportation * From {{ill|lt=Takatsuki Station|Q1071995|WD=Q1071995|1=Takatsuki Station (Osaka)}} on the {{ill|lt=JR Kyoto Line|Q1195820|WD=Q1195820|1=JR Kyōto Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Takatsuki City Transportation Department|Q11670759|WD=Q11670759|lt=Takatsuki municipal bus|1=Takatsuki City Bus}} toward Tsukawaki / Hara-Ōhashi, get off at "Uradō," then 3 minutes on foot ;Nearby *{{ill|lt=Akuto Shrine|Q11657135|WD=Q11657135|1=Akuto Shrine}} - the two shrines had deep ties from early times, including mutual mikoshi processions. *{{ill|lt=Yasuoka-ji (Takatsuki)|Q11450605|WD=Q11450605|lt=Yasuoka-ji|1=Yasuoka-ji}} - former {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} of Kamuhatori Shrine. === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===External links=== {{Commonscat|Jōgū-temmangū}} *[http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kamuhatori/index.htm Kamuhatori Shrine (official site)] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamuhatori Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamuhatori Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]]

== Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kamuhatori Shrine |画像=[[File:Kamihatori01.jpg|250px|]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|qid=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}Miyanokawara Motochō 2-25 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hinosuhaya-hi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mara no Sukune|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |別名= |札所等= |例祭= {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|qid=Q2550|1=May 5}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kamuhatori Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kamuhatori Shrine''' (かむはとりじんじゃ; now also read "Shinpuku Jinja") is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Miyanokawara Motochō, {{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|qid=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. ===Enshrined deities=== *Hinosuhaya-hi-no-Mikoto - distant ancestor of Mara no Sukune *Mara no Sukune - ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Hattori clan|Q11516960|qid=Q11516960|lt=Hattori no Muraji|1=Hattori no Muraji}}, an {{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11380553|qid=Q11380553|1=Isu no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} lineage descended from {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mihoko-no-Mikoto|Q119380365|qid=Q119380365|1=amenomihokonomikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} ===History=== [[File:Kamihatori02.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Honden]] To the northwest of the shrine at Tsukawaki, the "Hattori no Muraji mound" and "Meme-zuka" in a {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|qid=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} group are said to be the couple's graves of the Hattori no Muraji. The local Hattori {{ill|lt=muraji|Q2735611|qid=Q2735611|1=Muraji}} had worshiped their ancestral deity there and called it Hattori Shrine, but in the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|qid=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} era (901-923) it was renamed Kamuhatori Shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it was listed as one of three small shrines of {{ill|lt=Shimakami District|Q10965808|qid=Q10965808|1=Shimakami district}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q104843054|qid=Q104843054|1=維新}}, monks of {{ill|lt=Yasuoka-ji (Takatsuki)|Q11450605|qid=Q11450605|lt=Yasuoka-ji|1=Yasuoka-ji}} managed shrine affairs. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In November Meiji 40 (1907), Kasuga Shrine and Inari Shrine from Miyanohara Kawara were moved into the precincts. In the following year, Meiji 41 (1908), Jōgū Shrine of Tsukawaki, Wakamiya Shrine of Uradō, and Shinmei Shrine of Ōzōshi were {{ill|lt=merged shrine|Q11412524|qid=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}Yoshii Yoshitaka, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 5" (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. In Heisei 25 (2013), renovation work was completed[http://blog.seikikensetsu.co.jp/?eid=1422970 Renovation work completed for Kamuhatori Shrine! - Seiki Construction Co., Ltd.] accessed 2019-01-05. ===Precinct shrines=== *Kasuga Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qid=Q2564508}} *Inari Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} *Jōgū Shrine Hattori Ōmuraji-kō *Wakamiya Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qid=Q2564508}} *Shinmei Shrine {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} === Local information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|qid=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}Miyanokawara Motochō 2-25 ;Transportation * From {{ill|lt=Takatsuki Station|Q1071995|qid=Q1071995|1=Takatsuki Station (Osaka)}} on the {{ill|lt=JR Kyoto Line|Q1195820|qid=Q1195820|1=JR Kyōto Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Takatsuki City Transportation Department|Q11670759|qid=Q11670759|lt=Takatsuki municipal bus|1=Takatsuki City Bus}} toward Tsukawaki / Hara-Ōhashi, get off at "Uradō," then 3 minutes on foot ;Nearby *{{ill|lt=Akuto Shrine|Q11657135|qid=Q11657135|1=Akuto Shrine}} - the two shrines had deep ties from early times, including mutual mikoshi processions. *{{ill|lt=Yasuoka-ji (Takatsuki)|Q11450605|qid=Q11450605|lt=Yasuoka-ji|1=Yasuoka-ji}} - former {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|qid=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} of Kamuhatori Shrine. === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===External links=== {{Commonscat|Jōgū-temmangū}} *[http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kamuhatori/index.htm Kamuhatori Shrine (official site)] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamuhatori Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamuhatori Shrine]][[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]]
